summary: Published the developer portal at docs.lv3.org through the shared NGINX edge, reconciled the public DNS record, and verified live HTTPS responses for the generated documentation site.

verification.observed: Direct `dig` lookups against `1.1.1.1`, `8.8.8.8`, and `ns1.your-server.de` all resolved `docs.lv3.org` to `65.108.75.123`.

verification.check: Developer portal landing page over HTTPS
verification.observed: A `curl --resolve docs.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://docs.lv3.org` probe returned `HTTP/2 200` with `X-Robots-Tag: noindex, nofollow, noarchive`.
A `curl --resolve docs.lv3.org:443:65.108.75.123 https://docs.lv3.org/services/keycloak/` probe returned `HTTP/2 200` from the live edge.

notes: Current-main live rollout exposed and fixed three controller-side regressions before the final verification: a broken docs release-note link, the mutation-audit callback import path in worktree execution, and the subdomain record-name derivation in `playbooks/provision-subdomain.yml`.
The shared edge role also needed the Proxmox-host topology variables injected in its defaults so `platform_service_topology` could render correctly on `nginx-lv3` during live publication.
During investigation, an incorrect full-FQDN Hetzner record was created and then removed directly through the Hetzner DNS API before the corrected repo-managed workflow was rerun.

The controller machine's local resolver remained stale after public DNS propagated, so HTTPS verification used `curl --resolve` against the published edge IP while DNS propagation was confirmed with external resolvers.
